skill.name: maxhub-twitter
skill.description: Twitter/X platform skill for tweet search, user data, and trend analysis. Bilingual support (Chinese/English). Activates when users mention twitter, x, tweet, or topic-related needs.
skill.summary: Twitter/X API integration via MaxHub for tweet search, user analysis, and trend tracking
skill.author: MaxHub Team

security_audit.summary: All 85 static findings are false positives. The 'external_commands' flags (55 locations) incorrectly identified API documentation paths in markdown as shell commands. 'env_access' flags are legitimate environment variable usage for API authentication. 'network' flags reference only the trusted MaxHub API service. No malicious intent detected. Skill is safe for publication.

content.user_title: Search Twitter/X Content and Analyze Trends
content.value_statement: Users need to search tweets, analyze user data, and track trending topics. This skill provides structured access to Twitter/X data through MaxHub API with built-in safety limits.
actual_capabilities: Search tweets by keyword via Twitter/X API
Fetch user profiles and follower data
Retrieve tweet details, replies, and retweet users
Chain multiple API calls for complex analysis
Batch data collection with pagination control
limitations: Requires MaxHub API key (not direct Twitter API)
Read-only access - cannot post, like, or follow
Rate limited by MaxHub API quotas
No local data storage - returns data only

use_cases.title: Social Media Manager Research
use_cases.description: Track competitor accounts, analyze engagement metrics, and identify trending topics in your industry.

use_cases.title: Academic Data Collection
use_cases.description: Collect public tweet data for research on social media trends, sentiment analysis, and network patterns.

use_cases.title: Content Creator Inspiration
use_cases.description: Find popular tweets by topic, analyze what content resonates with audiences, and track hashtag performance.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Tweet Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for recent tweets about {topic} on Twitter
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to find content by topic

prompt_templates.title: User Profile Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Twitter account: @{username}. Get their follower count, recent tweets, and engagement metrics.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to research a specific account

prompt_templates.title: Trend Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the trending topics on Twitter right now? Show me the top 10 with tweet counts.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to track current trends

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Reference Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Find tweets about {topic}, then get details on the top 5 most engaged posts and their authors.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user needs multi-step data gathering

output_examples.input: Search Twitter for tweets about AI research

best_practices: Start with specific search terms to avoid overwhelming results
Use pagination limits (max 5 pages) for large datasets
Check account balance before large batch operations
anti_patterns: Do not request unlimited data without pagination
Do not share your MaxHub API key with others
Do not use for automated spam or bulk unrelated posting

faq.question: Do I need a Twitter developer account?
faq.answer: No. This skill uses MaxHub API which handles Twitter API access for you. You only need a MaxHub account and API key.

faq.question: Can I post tweets with this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides read-only access for searching, analyzing, and collecting data. No posting, liking, or following.

faq.question: Is there a rate limit?
faq.answer: Yes. MaxHub API has default limits of 60 calls per minute and maximum 5 pages per request.

faq.question: What data does this skill access?
faq.answer: Public tweet data including content, user profiles, engagement metrics, and trending topics. No private DMs or protected accounts.

faq.question: How is my API key protected?
faq.answer: The key is passed via environment variable MAXHUB_API_KEY and is never stored, logged, or shared with third parties.

faq.question: Can I analyze my own account?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide your username and the skill can fetch your public profile and posts for analysis.
